title: aphanamixis polillensis
intro: Aphanamixis polillensis, also known as Atis or Quek, is a plant species native to the Philippines and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Meliaceae. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to the presence of bioactive compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Aphanamixis polillensis include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of various diseases, and its potential to lower cholesterol levels and reduce the risk of cardiovascular diseases. Aphanamixis polillensis is traditionally prepared as a decoction or infusion, and its leaves and bark are used to make herbal teas, capsules, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments.
